feedback.details: Diagram validates successfully with mmdc (exit code 0). All 8 relationships render without errors. No syntax issues detected.

Diagram contains exactly: 1 System box (llm-switch, line 8), 2 Person_Ext boxes (Developers line 6, Operations Engineers line 7), and exactly 6 System_Ext boxes (Nomad line 9, Consul line 10, Vault line 11, Local Model Servers line 12, Frontier API Providers line 13, Observability Tools line 14). No Container or Component boxes present. Correct C4 Level 1 composition.

All 6 external systems have labeled relationships with protocols: nomad ('Nomad job specification', line 18), consul ('HTTP/DNS', line 19), vault ('HTTP/TLS', line 20), localmodels ('HTTP/gRPC', line 21), frontierapis ('HTTP', line 22), observability ('HTTP/gRPC', line 23). Additionally, both user roles have labeled connections (lines 16-17). No unlabeled connections.

File contains exactly four H2 sections with adequate word counts: 'System Context' (line 28, ~256 words, threshold 150 met), 'User Roles' (line 38, ~133 words, threshold 100 met), 'External Dependencies' (line 43, ~180 words, threshold 100 met), 'Key Interactions' (line 55, ~170 words, threshold 150 met). All use proper H2 headers.

All diagram elements include PRD references (lines 6-14). All narrative paragraphs end with PRD references (e.g., [PRD-Executive Summary] line 29, [PRD-FR1, FR2] line 56). Minor deduction: some references could be more granular (e.g., [PRD-Domain-Specific Requirements - Operational Excellence & Observability] at line 9 is lengthy but valid; consider using feature requirement IDs like FR12, FR16 for precision).

All 7 required technologies explicitly listed: Golang ('Go service' line 32), Docker ('Docker containers' line 32), Nomad (lines 32, 45, 57), Consul (lines 32, 46, 58), Vault (lines 32, 47, 58), vLLM (line 33, 48), langfuse (line 34, 50). Diagram uses correct C4 shapes: Person_Ext (line 6-7), System (line 8), System_Ext (lines 9-14). No extraneous technology boxes.

All five edge cases explicitly documented: (1) Zero-code-change: line 41 'zero-code-change integration pattern' and line 56. (2) Multiple model types: lines 12-13 distinction between local models and frontier APIs, line 33, line 59-60. (3) Nomad constraints: line 57 'placement constraints favoring GPU-equipped nodes', 'resource limits', 'health check configurations'. (4) API timeout scenarios: line 35 'configurable timeouts and circuit breaker patterns', line 53, line 62. (5) Network partition tolerance: line 36, line 52 'handle Consul or Vault network partitions', line 63.

File begins with H1 '# C1 System Context' (line 1), followed immediately by Mermaid diagram block (lines 3-26), followed by narrative sections (line 28+). No extraneous text between H1 and diagram. Correct ordering maintained.

All diagram labels match exactly with narrative terms (case-sensitive): 'Developers' (line 6, 39, 56), 'Operations Engineers' (line 7, 40, 57), 'llm-switch' (line 8, 29, 44, etc.), 'Nomad' (line 9, 32, 45, 57), 'Consul' (line 10, 32, 46, 58, 63), 'Vault' (line 11, 32, 47, 58, 63), 'Local Model Servers' (line 12, 48, 59), 'Frontier API Providers' (line 13, 49, 60), 'Observability Tools' (line 14, 50, 61). No abbreviation discrepancies.

overall_summary: The C1 System Context document is production-ready with comprehensive coverage of all criteria. The Mermaid diagram validates successfully, correctly implements C4 Level 1 composition (1 system, 2 people, 6 external systems), and all relationships are labeled with protocols. The narrative meets all word count requirements across the four mandatory H2 sections. PRD traceability is thorough with references attached to every diagram element and narrative paragraph. Technology stack compliance is complete with all 7 required technologies explicitly named. Edge case coverage is excellent, explicitly addressing zero-code-change integration, local vs frontier model handling, Nomad deployment constraints (resource limits, placement constraints, health checks), API timeout scenarios with circuit breakers, and network partition tolerance for Consul/Vault. Label consistency is perfect with exact case-sensitive matches between diagram and narrative. The one minor improvement opportunity is PRD reference granularity—some references could use specific feature requirement IDs (FR#) rather than section titles for tighter traceability.
